pr.title: Attempt to resolve the mystery of HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: PR 2577 renamed the text symbol/scratch entry ftCo_800C6AFC at 0x800C6AFC to HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty, moved its declaration to baselib/jobj.h, and replaced many TU-local fake HSD_JObjSet* helpers with canonical jobj.h helpers or newly added WithMtxDirty variants. The PR body frames this as cleanup of duplicated fake helpers after investigating why JObj helpers were calling a fighter-range function. Review accepted the cleanup while noting the macro/parentheses behavior was weird and that the macro itself was fake.

key_files.role: Added HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty declaration and a family of WithMtxDirty helper inlines plus out-of-line dirty-call wrappers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:jobj.h HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty declaration and WithMtxDirty additions

key_files.role: Renamed .text:0x800C6AFC from ftCo_800C6AFC to HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty.
Renamed scratch OK entries at 0x800C6AFC from ftCo_800C6AFC to HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty.
Renamed ftCo_800C6AFC definition to parenthesized HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty and replaced fake translate helper with HSD_JObjSetTranslateWithMtxDirtyOutOfLine.
Removed the ftCo_800C6AFC fighter header prototype after moving identity to jobj.h.
Removed Kirby-local fake JObj get/set helpers and replaced call sites with HSD_JObjGetScale, HSD_JObjSetScale, and HSD_JObjSetRotationY.
Deleted fake tyfall HSD_JObjSet* helper header that routed mtx dirty through ftCo_800C6AFC.
Removed static fake helper include and ft_0C31 include; replaced fake tyfall JObj calls with WithMtxDirty helpers.
Replaced fake tyfall JObj set helpers with WithMtxDirty helpers.
Removed local fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate and used HSD_JObjSetTranslateWithMtxDirty.
Changed a fake scale helper's dirty call from ftCo_800C6AFC to parenthesized HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty.
Replaced fake translate/rotation preservation helpers with WithMtxDirty helpers.
Removed fake JObj helper definitions and the ft_0C31 include.
Changed four JObj helper call sites in the broader cleanup.
Changed one JObj helper call hunk in the broader cleanup.
Changed three JObj helper hunks; bot report later noted an unmatched-item regression in it_802EDDC0.
Replaced fake translate/rotation/scale helpers on linkarrow bones with WithMtxDirty helpers.
Renamed a local inline to AnimAddWithMtxDirty and replaced fake add-translation/add-rotation helpers.
Changed one JObj helper call hunk in the broader cleanup.
Changed three JObj helper hunks in the broader cleanup.
Changed four JObj helper hunks in the broader cleanup.
Changed seven JObj helper hunks in the broader cleanup.
Changed seven JObj helper hunks in the broader cleanup.
Changed seven JObj helper hunks in the broader cleanup.

what_changed: The project metadata for 0x800C6AFC was renamed from ftCo_800C6AFC to HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty in symbols.txt and scratches.txt.
The ft_0C31.c function definition was renamed from ftCo_800C6AFC to void (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(HSD_JObj* jobj), and the ft_0C31.h prototype was removed.
jobj.h gained a declaration for void(HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(HSD_JObj* jobj), HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtyOutOfLineLeaf, HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtyOutOfLine, and multiple HSD_JObj*WithMtxDirty inline helpers.
TU-local fake helpers that duplicated jobj.h assert lines and JObj field writes were removed from several fighter, game-mode, stage, and item files.

Call sites were switched either to existing canonical helpers such as HSD_JObjSetScale/HSD_JObjGetScale/HSD_JObjSetRotationY or to new WithMtxDirty variants when direct dirty-call behavior was needed.
gmregtyfall.static.h was deleted after its fake tyfall helper definitions became unnecessary.

A bot report on the PR slice recorded a matched-code decrease, one broken match in ftKb_PrSpecialAirN_Anim, and one unmatched-item regression in itkyasarin/it_802EDDC0; the slice does not show a later bot report resolving these.

smart_moves.text: Centralized duplicated HSD_JObjSet* logic in baselib/jobj.h instead of keeping subsystem-local fake helpers.
evidence_refs: PR body: 'replace most of the fake helpers with the real deal'

smart_moves.text: Updated symbol metadata and scratch labels together with the source rename, reducing the chance of source/config drift around 0x800C6AFC.
Used WithMtxDirty helper names to make the unusual direct dirty-call behavior explicit at call sites instead of hiding it behind fake subsystem-specific helper names.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When many TUs contain local fake copies of a header inline, compare the assert file/line numbers and field writes against the owning header helper; if they match, cleanup should usually move toward the canonical header helper or an explicitly named shared variant.
A function emitted in an unexpected TU range can still be a header/library helper body if linker order and first call site explain where the compiler emitted the out-of-line copy.
evidence_refs: review:ribbanya comment about linker order and first call site
PR body: ftCo_800C6AFC concluded to be HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty

decomp_lessons.lesson: If a fake helper is needed only to route a dirty-call in a specific codegen shape, prefer a clearly named shared helper such as a WithMtxDirty or OutOfLine variant over duplicated local fake helpers.

naming_conventions.name: HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty
naming_conventions.convention: Use the baselib JObj helper name for the 0x800C6AFC function instead of the fighter-address-style name ftCo_800C6AFC.

naming_conventions.name: HSD_JObj*WithMtxDirty
naming_conventions.convention: Suffix JObj helper variants with WithMtxDirty when the helper performs the field write and calls HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty directly rather than using the existing normal helper shape.

naming_conventions.name: HSD_JObjSetTranslateWithMtxDirtyOutOfLine
naming_conventions.convention: Use an OutOfLine suffix for the variant that routes the dirty call through HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtyOutOfLine rather than directly through the parenthesized HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty call.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Parenthesize the HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty identifier at definition/call sites, e.g. void (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(...) and (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(jobj), to avoid macro expansion behavior or force different macro handling.
evidence_refs: review:ribbanya question about wrapping the called function
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Reviewer said the macro is fake and accepted the weird parentheses only as part of cleaning up duplicates; do not generalize this as normal style without fresh matching evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use two-step out-of-line leaf wrappers around HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty when call/inline shape must differ from direct inline dirty calls.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR body says attempts to unify everything failed; the slice does not include detailed objdiff reasoning for each remaining variant.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replace local __assert/HSD_ASSERT expansions that identify jobj.h line numbers with shared jobj.h inline helpers.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: This aligns with loaded header-inline standards, but final per-function match status is mixed in the available bot report.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Asked why the called function was wrapped in parentheses and suggested that, if the function is in jobj.h and ft_0C31.c contains the first call site by linker order and it is not fit to be inlined, the compiler may emit the function body to that TU.

review_feedback.author: lukechampine
review_feedback.feedback: Reported that wrapping a macro in parentheses when calling it results in different behavior versus calling it unwrapped.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Stated that the macro is fake and approved merging the cleaned-up duplicates for now, even with weird parentheses.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.feedback: Initial report showed matched code 70.08% (-0.04%, -1380 bytes), one broken match in ftKb_PrSpecialAirN_Anim, and one unmatched-item regression in itkyasarin/it_802EDDC0.

follow_up_queries: Search for remaining calls or declarations of ftCo_800C6AFC after PR 2577.
Search for HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty macro definitions and why parenthesized calls differ from unwrapped calls.
Search for remaining fake_HSD_JObjSet* or fake_tyfall_HSD_JObjSet* helpers after this cleanup.
Search later PRs touching HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty, WithMtxDirty helpers, or jobj.h macro cleanup.
Check whether later bot reports or commits resolved the ftKb_PrSpecialAirN_Anim and itkyasarin regressions noted in the initial PR report.

notes: Changed-file metadata and several relevant diff hunks are strong evidence for the symbol rename, fake-helper deletion, and jobj.h helper additions.
The raw diff loaded in the prompt is truncated, so some changed files are represented only by changed_files metadata rather than exact hunks.
The PR body explicitly says Codex investigated and could not unify all cases; treat broader causal claims about compiler behavior as review hypotheses unless backed by later source evidence.
The reviewer approved merging cleaned-up duplicates but also called the macro fake and the parentheses weird; do not treat the parenthesized macro trick as an accepted global style.
The available bot report shows regressions; no later loaded evidence proves they were resolved before merge.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Centralize fake HSD_JObjSet* duplicates in jobj.h when assert-line evidence matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2577 is evidence that local fake HSD_JObjSet* helpers with jobj.h assert lines and identical JObj field writes can often be replaced by canonical jobj.h helpers or explicitly named shared variants, rather than keeping duplicated subsystem-local fake helpers.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header helper emitted in unexpected TU range
accepted_candidate_records.text: Reviewer feedback in PR 2577 records a plausible matching explanation: a jobj.h helper body may be emitted into ft_0C31.c if linker order makes that TU contain the first call site and the function is not fit to inline.

source_update_candidates.title: 0x800C6AFC renamed from ftCo_800C6AFC to HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2577 changes config/GALE01/symbols.txt and scratches.txt so the function at .text/OK 0x800C6AFC is named HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty instead of ftCo_800C6AFC, and removes the ft_0C31.h prototype for the old fighter-scoped name.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a source-owned symbol/path fact and should be curated separately from postmortem lessons.

source_update_candidates.title: Treat parenthesized function-like macro calls as a narrow matching tactic, not normal style
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2577 used forms like void (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(...) and (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(jobj); the author noted wrapping a macro in parentheses changed behavior, while the reviewer said the macro was fake and accepted the cleanup despite weird parentheses. Candidate standard: parenthesized macro/function identifiers may be used only with explicit local matching evidence and should not become ordinary style.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR provides useful cautionary evidence, but the reviewer explicitly called the macro fake; curator should decide whether this belongs under matching-tactics or avoid-pragmas/register-asm-style exception guidance.

source_update_candidates.title: Prefer shared header helpers over duplicated local fake HSD_JObjSet* copies
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2577 removed multiple fake JObj helpers that copied jobj.h asserts and field writes, replacing them with canonical HSD_JObj helpers or shared WithMtxDirty variants. Candidate standard extension for header-inlines: when a fake local helper mirrors a header inline, move toward the owning header/helper layer rather than duplicating the body in TUs.
source_update_candidates.reason: This likely reinforces the existing header-inlines standard but should be reviewed for wording and scope.

rejection_notes: Do not promote 'ftCo_800C6AFC was actually HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty' as a universal inference pattern from AI/Codex alone; in this slice it is supported by merged symbol/source edits and reviewer discussion, but future cases still need source/linker evidence.

Do not promote the WithMtxDirty helper family as proof that every normal HSD_JObjSet* call should be replaced; the diff keeps both normal helpers and WithMtxDirty variants depending on local codegen needs.
Do not claim final regression-free validation from this slice; the loaded bot report shows regressions and no loaded later report clears them.
Do not infer reviewer intent beyond the explicit approval/comment text: the reviewer was fine merging cleaned-up duplicates for now, not necessarily endorsing weird parentheses as preferred style.
